ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code for the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the countries table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "provinces" (
  "province_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each province */,
  "province_name" TEXT /* Name of the province */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the departments table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("province_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_provinces_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "districts" (
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each district */,
  "district_name" TEXT /* Name of the district */,
  "province_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the provinces table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("district_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_districts_province_id FOREIGN KEY ("province_id") REFERENCES provinces ("province_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the districts table */,
  "ubigeo" INTEGER /* UBIGEO code for the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_regions_district_id FOREIGN KEY ("district_id") REFERENCES districts ("district_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographic_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the data was collected */,
  "week" TEXT /* Week number for the data point (in the format YYYY-WW) */,
  "uuid" INTEGER /* Unique identifier (UUID) for the data point */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the region */,
  "male_population" INTEGER /* Male population of the region */,
  "female_population" INTEGER /* Female population of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographic_data_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, analyst, researcher) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the demographic data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the data was accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES demographic_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user with the permission */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the demographic data with the permission */,
  "read_permission" INTEGER /* Read permission flag */,
  "write_permission" INTEGER /* Write permission flag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES demographic_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
